description:
Make each typed closure stub allocate the callee body's frame keyed by ITS SpecId (not the any-key FnId.0).

## What

In `compile_closure_stub` (called from `src/ir_codegen.rs:1722`), the call:
  `callee_sid = callee_fn_id.0; // any-key invariant`
becomes:
  `callee_sid = self_sid; // the stub's own SpecId from .29.12.2 keying`

- The stub coerces captures using `param_reprs[self_sid][0..n_caps]`.
- The stub coerces args using `param_reprs[self_sid][n_caps..]`.
- The stub allocates `schemas[self_sid]` and writes `header.schema_id = self_sid`.
- The stub returns the frame to the trampoline, which dispatches via `fn_ptrs[self_sid]`.

## Why

This is the actual typed dispatch. After .29.12.2 we have per-SpecId stubs; this ticket makes each stub point at its narrow body.

## Tests (TDD)

- Closure that closes over an int and gets called with int args dispatches to a narrow body that the typer specialized. Assert via fingerprint (e.g., printed int_lit narrowing visible in output, or a counter increment that only the narrow body performs).
- Three-path parity (interp/JIT/AOT).
title: Closure stub body dispatches to its SpecId
